title: New Rules for Food Waste in England
summary: In England, new rules started that say all homes must have their food waste collected every week. This helps the environment by turning food waste into electricity instead of letting it rot in landfills, which makes bad gases. But many councils, which are local groups in charge of waste, are not ready yet. Some councils do not have the right trucks or enough money to start this service. The government gave over £340 million to help, but still, about one in four councils missed the deadline. Some councils plan to start by 2026, while others do not know when they will begin. This is a big change in recycling, and it hopes to make people waste less food too.

background_read: In England, councils are local government groups that handle services like collecting trash and recycling. Food waste is leftover food that can be harmful if thrown in landfills because it rots and releases gases that hurt the environment. Recycling food waste separately helps turn it into electricity, which is a cleaner energy source. The government made new rules to make this happen every week for all homes, but some councils are struggling to start because they need special trucks and more money. This is part of a bigger effort to reduce waste and protect the planet.
Article_Structure: WHO: Councils in England and the government (Defra)
WHAT: New rules for weekly food waste collection
WHY: To reduce waste, make electricity, and help the environment

perspectives.description: The government wants to improve recycling and reduce environmental harm by enforcing new rules.
Some councils face challenges like lack of funding and vehicles, causing delays in starting the service.
